frameworks.name: fMRI multivoxel pattern decoding
frameworks.one_line: decoded representations of feared objects identified from multivoxel fMRI patterns

frameworks.name: decoded neurofeedback / unconscious reinforcement
frameworks.one_line: monetary reward paired with unconscious occurrences of feared-object brain patterns

frameworks.name: hyperalignment across subjects
frameworks.one_line: fear representations inferred for a target subject using surrogate participants

frameworks.name: exposure therapy
frameworks.one_line: standard aversive intervention with high premature attrition this method aims to bypass

core_question: Can hardwired fear responses to objects like spiders and snakes be reprogrammed unconsciously in the human brain?
key_assumptions: feared-object representations are decodable from multivoxel fMRI patterns
representations transfer across individuals via hyperalignment
pairing reward with unconscious neural representations can modify fear
manipulations: pairing of monetary reward with unconscious decoded fear representations
use of surrogate-derived representations instead of explicit image exposure
key_findings: method reliably reduced physiological fear responses measured by skin conductance
amygdala hemodynamic activity to feared objects was reduced
fear decoding was achieved without conscious presentation of feared images
interpretations: unconscious decoded neurofeedback can bypass the aversiveness of conscious exposure
hyperalignment-based decoding enables clinically tractable fear reprogramming
